_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Stenosis of nasolacrimal duct refractory to conventional treatment such as warm compresses, massage, medication and repeated probing of the duct.

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, indications for doing the procedure & advise for admission)
Examination/ investigation findings- Dye disappearance test/ Probing & irrigation

claim.label: Intraoperative photograph with time and date (Optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes (including clinical signs & symptoms such as excessive watering of eyes, swelling around the lacrimal duct& advise for admission) present?
Are examination/investigation findings such as - dye disappearance test/ probing & irrigation findings present?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of the surgery?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Are intra operative endoscopic still images submitted?

common_queries: Clinical examination findings do not confirm chronic or long-standing epiphora.
Investigations performed do not confirm chronic dacryocystitis or the need for DCR.
Missing or incomplete clinical notes detailing signs, symptoms, indications for procedure, or advice for admission.
Missing or incomplete examination/investigation findings, such as dye disappearance test, probing, or irrigation findings.
Missing or incomplete detailed discharge summary.
Missing or incomplete procedure note or operative note.
Intraoperative endoscopic still images not submitted or missing time and date.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination and does not respond to conventional medical therapy.

Endoscopic dacryocystorhinostomy (DCR) is a minimally invasive technique used to treat patients diagnosed with nasolacrimal duct obstruction (NLDO). This can be caused by chronic infection or trauma and can also be congenital or acquired.
